input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (which would be the next version after .NET 9).

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. Starting with Android 11+ and recent MAUI versions, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in the Android platform itself. In .NET 10 (MAUI), the recommended approach changed.

Let me provide the correct guidance:

In Android, `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated in API 30 (Android 11). The modern replacement is to use `WindowInsets` APIs, specifically edge-to-edge display with inset handling.


For .NET 10, the solution is to leverage `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which has been available since .NET 8 and handles keyboard overlap automatically, or properly configure edge-to-edge display with inset handling to account for the keyboard appearing on screen.
